reasoning: The hypothesis `∀ x y, |f x - f y| ≤ |x - y| ^ 2` captures the informal condition `|f(t)-f(x)| ≤ |t-x|^2` for all t, x exactly (the variable names t, x vs x, y are immaterial). The conclusion `∃ c, f = λ _x => c` is the standard encoding of "f is a constant function" — by function extensionality it is equivalent to `∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c`, which is exactly "f is constant". The domain and codomain are both `ℝ`, matching the informal setup. No hypotheses have been added or dropped, and there is no narrowing or strengthening of the conclusion.
